problem: The function $f$, defined on the set of ordered pairs of positive integers, satisfies the following properties: \begin{align*}  f(x,x) &=x, \\  f(x,y) &=f(y,x), \quad \text{and} \\ (x + y) f(x,y) &= yf(x,x + y).  \end{align*}Calculate $f(14,52)$.

classified_questions.question: What is the function $f$ defined on?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What are the properties satisfied by the function $f$?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How can we rewrite the third equation to make it easier to work with?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Under what condition can we apply the rewritten equation?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How does applying the rewritten equation repeatedly help us compute $f(x, t)$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the final expression we arrive at for $f(x, t)$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How do we use the properties of $f$ to compute $f(14, 52)$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What arguments did we use to compute $f(14,10)$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the final answer for $f(14,52)$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can this method be applied to compute other values of $f$?
classified_questions.label: b
